questions.question_text: What is the name of New Hampshire's primary anti-discrimination statute for real estate?
questions.explanation: RSA 354-A is New Hampshire's Law Against Discrimination, which provides protections in housing that go beyond the federal Fair Housing Act by adding state-specific protected classes.

questions.question_text: Which protected classes does NH add beyond the seven federal Fair Housing Act classes?
distractors: Only veteran status and source of income
questions.explanation: RSA 354-A extends protections beyond the seven federal classes to include age, marital status, sexual orientation, and gender identity. Income level, veteran status, and citizenship are not NH-specific additions.

questions.question_text: A landlord refuses to rent to a couple because they are unmarried. Under RSA 354-A, is this lawful?
questions.correct_answer: No, marital status is protected in NH
distractors: Only if the property has 5+ units
questions.explanation: RSA 354-A protects marital status as a protected class. Refusing to rent based on whether a couple is married or unmarried constitutes illegal discrimination in New Hampshire.

questions.question_text: Gender identity is a protected class in NH real estate transactions under which statute?
questions.explanation: RSA 354-A, New Hampshire's Law Against Discrimination, includes gender identity as a protected class in housing. This protection is a state-level addition beyond federal Fair Housing Act protections.

questions.question_text: An agent refuses to show homes in a certain area to buyers over age 50. Under NH law, what violation has occurred?
distractors: No violation if area is unsafe
questions.explanation: Age is a protected class under RSA 354-A. Refusing to show homes in certain areas based on a buyer's age constitutes both age discrimination and steering, which are prohibited practices.

questions.question_text: Under RSA 354-A:12, which practice involves inducing owners to sell by claiming a protected class is moving into the area?
questions.explanation: RSA 354-A:12 prohibits blockbusting, which involves inducing property owners to sell by making representations about the entry of members of a protected class into the neighborhood.

questions.question_text: A property manager tells a prospective tenant that no units are available, but units are actually vacant. Under RSA 354-A, what violation is this?
questions.explanation: RSA 354-A prohibits representing that a dwelling is not available for sale or rent when it actually is, if done on the basis of a protected class. This false unavailability claim is a prohibited discriminatory practice.

questions.question_text: An agent offers less favorable loan terms to buyers of a particular national origin. Under RSA 354-A, this is an example of what?
distractors: A permissible local market rate adjustment
questions.explanation: RSA 354-A prohibits offering discriminatory terms or conditions in real estate transactions based on protected class membership. Varying loan terms by national origin is illegal discrimination.

questions.question_text: Which practice involves directing buyers toward or away from neighborhoods based on race?
questions.explanation: Steering involves directing prospective buyers or renters toward or away from certain neighborhoods based on protected class characteristics such as race. This is prohibited under both RSA 354-A and federal fair housing law.

questions.question_text: A real estate agent tells homeowners their property values will drop because families of a certain religion are buying nearby. What violation is this?
distractors: Protected free speech under the law
questions.explanation: Under RSA 354-A:12, inducing owners to sell by claiming property values will decline because members of a protected class are entering the area constitutes blockbusting, a prohibited practice.

questions.question_text: Which agency investigates housing discrimination complaints in New Hampshire?
questions.explanation: The New Hampshire Commission for Human Rights is the state agency responsible for investigating complaints of housing discrimination under RSA 354-A.

questions.question_text: An owner lives in a duplex and rents the other unit. Under NH fair housing law, which exemption may apply?
distractors: No exemptions exist in NH law
questions.explanation: The Mrs. Murphy exemption applies to owner-occupied properties with up to 4 units. A duplex where the owner lives in one unit may qualify for this exemption from certain fair housing requirements.

questions.question_text: Which organizations may have limited fair housing exemptions in NH?
distractors: All licensed real estate brokerage companies
questions.explanation: Religious organizations and private clubs may have limited exemptions from certain fair housing requirements when providing housing to their members. These exemptions are narrow and do not permit discrimination based on race.

questions.question_text: What remedies are available to a victim of housing discrimination under RSA 354-A?
distractors: Only a formal written apology is required
Only the license suspension of the agent
Only a small claims court filing is allowed
questions.explanation: Under RSA 354-A, remedies for housing discrimination include actual damages, injunctive relief, civil penalties, and attorney fees. The range of remedies is broad to deter discriminatory conduct.

questions.question_text: A landlord who owns a five-unit building and lives in one unit claims the Mrs. Murphy exemption. Is this valid?
questions.correct_answer: No, the exemption covers up to 4 units
distractors: Yes, if no agent is involved
questions.explanation: The Mrs. Murphy exemption applies only to owner-occupied properties with up to 4 units. A five-unit building exceeds this threshold, so the owner cannot claim this exemption regardless of occupancy.
